headline_summary: Reuters (Apr 4): Trump issues a 48-hour ultimatum for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz; Israel signals readiness to strike Iranian energy facilities, raising near-term escalation risk.

Reuters (Apr 4): Iran authorizes passage for vessels carrying essential goods to Iranian ports via Hormuz under coordination/protocols, implying managed/conditional transits rather than full reopening.
Reuters (Apr 3): U.S. intelligence assesses Iran is unlikely to ease its effective Strait of Hormuz chokehold soon, sustaining disruption risk at a key oil chokepoint.
Reuters (Apr 3): Drone attack hits Kuwait’s Mina al-Ahmadi refinery; UK deploys Rapid Sentry air defence system to Kuwait amid heightened Gulf infrastructure risk.
Reuters (Apr 1): UKMTO reports a tanker struck off Doha; multi-front attacks (Kuwait, Bahrain) underline maritime and infrastructure vulnerability.
Reuters (Apr 3): First Japan-linked LNG tanker crosses the Strait of Hormuz since the war began; dozens of Japan-linked vessels remain stranded, indicating selective transits.
Reuters (Apr 2): Countries and the EU push back on any 'pay-to-pass' regime and seek reopening plans; diplomacy continues alongside threats.
Reuters (Apr 2): Legal/political backlash grows in the U.S. over strikes on Iran, adding constraints/uncertainty around campaign duration and escalation pathways.
risk_drivers: Strait of Hormuz managed-closure risk (permits, fees, intermittent transit) and uncertain duration
Rising probability of strikes on Iranian energy/power infrastructure following ultimatum dynamics
Gulf energy and transport infrastructure exposure (Kuwait refinery/airport; Bahrain facility fires)
Maritime security incidents (tanker hit off Doha; vessel fires in/near Hormuz) and insurance/freight shocks
Air incidents/search-and-rescue friction (downed aircraft, missing crew) increasing escalation risk

Energy: oil_up_risk: elevated geopolitical premium persists while Hormuz access remains constrained/conditional
Prompt spreads/backwardation risk if physical flows tighten or insurers further restrict cover
Refined products risk: Gulf refinery outages or power disruptions can lift cracks and raise regional product volatility
FX: em_fx_pressure: higher energy import bills pressure energy-importer EM FX and can widen current-account concerns
risk_off_flows: escalation headlines support USD/CHF/JPY; pressure on high-beta and MENA frontier markets
Rates: rates_credit_spreads: risk-off and inflation uncertainty can widen HY/EM spreads; oil-linked breakevens may rise
Term premia can reprice higher if energy-driven inflation shocks appear persistent
Equities: risk_off_flows: downside for cyclicals, airlines, shipping/transport cost-exposed sectors; relative support for energy/defense
Regional equity volatility rises in GCC markets amid infrastructure-strike risk

sources.title: Trump, Israel pressure Iran ahead of deadline as hunt goes on for missing US crew member
Iran allows essential goods vessels to its ports via Hormuz strait, Tasnim says
Exclusive: US intelligence warns Iran unlikely to ease Hormuz Strait chokehold soon, sources say
Drones hit Kuwait's Mina al-Ahmadi refinery, fires reported, no injuries
UK deploying Rapid Sentry air defence system to Kuwait, PM's office says
US eyes swift end to war as fresh attacks hit Gulf states and Iran
Japanese-owned LNG tanker crosses the Strait of Hormuz
World anxious to open Hormuz Strait while Trump and Iran trade threats
US experts say American strikes on Iran may amount to war crimes

events.title: U.S. intelligence assesses Iran unlikely to ease Strait of Hormuz chokehold soon
events.implication: Prolonged disruption risk to ~20% of global oil transit supports elevated crude risk premium.

events.title: Iran authorizes 'essential goods' vessels to Iranian ports to transit the Strait of Hormuz under protocols (Reuters)
events.implication: Indicates conditional/managed transits rather than full reopening; supports continued disruption/insurance risk premia.

events.title: Iran uses attacks, mines and passage fees to effectively block Hormuz transit, Reuters reports
events.implication: Reduced insurability and throughput implies supply shock and higher freight/insurance costs.

events.title: U.S. intelligence says Iran unlikely to ease Strait of Hormuz chokehold soon (Reuters)
events.implication: Sustained chokepoint constraints keep tanker delays/insurance elevated; reinforces near-term oil risk premium.

events.title: U.S. intelligence warns Iran unlikely to ease its Strait of Hormuz chokehold soon (Reuters)
events.implication: Longer disruption window supports sustained oil risk premium and tight prompt physical conditions.

events.title: Trump vows intensified strikes on Iran over next 2-3 weeks; markets turn risk-off
events.implication: Escalatory guidance increases tail risk of further hits to Iranian energy infrastructure; supports oil risk premium and risk-off cross-asset moves.

events.title: Iran shoots down U.S. F-15E over southwestern Iran; one crew member missing
events.implication: Downing of U.S. aircraft increases escalation and retaliation risk, elevating threat to regional assets.

events.title: U.S. aircraft downed over Iran/Gulf; missing pilot search escalates tensions (Reuters)
events.implication: Direct U.S.-Iran kinetic incidents raise rapid-escalation and retaliation risk, feeding oil and risk-off volatility.

events.title: Trump issues 48-hour ultimatum for Iran to open Hormuz; Israel says prepared to attack Iranian energy facilities pending U.S. go-ahead (Reuters)
events.implication: Near-term escalation risk rises, especially around Iranian energy/power targets; markets likely to price higher tail-risk into oil and risk assets.

events.title: Iranian military spokesperson says Hormuz closed 'long term' to U.S. and Israel
events.implication: Explicit closure stance sustains crude supply shock and elevates geopolitical risk premia.

events.title: Drones hit Kuwait’s Mina al-Ahmadi refinery; fires reported (Reuters)
events.implication: Raises risk of refined product supply disruptions and reinforces vulnerability of Gulf energy infrastructure.

events.title: Iranian and U.S. forces search for missing U.S. crew member after downing of a U.S. warplane; separate U.S. aircraft crash over Kuwait reported (Reuters)
events.implication: Search-and-rescue dynamics and personnel recovery risks can drive retaliatory actions and further air-defense/strike escalation.

events.title: Drone strike hits Mina al-Ahmadi refinery; fires reported, no injuries
events.implication: Direct hit on refining assets elevates risk premium in refined products and regional supply reliability.

events.title: UN Security Council to vote on Bahrain-drafted resolution to protect shipping around the Strait of Hormuz
events.location_name: United Nations Headquarters, New York, United States
events.implication: Potential authorization for defensive measures could change escalation dynamics and maritime risk pricing; veto threats highlight great-power disagreement.

events.title: Iran says it is drafting a protocol with Oman requiring ship permits and licenses for Hormuz transit
events.implication: Institutionalizing control over transit can function as de facto restriction and keep insurance/freight elevated.

events.title: UN Security Council to vote on Bahrain-drafted resolution to protect commercial shipping around Hormuz
events.location_name: United Nations Headquarters, New York, United States
events.implication: If passed, could enable expanded maritime protection; great-power disagreement raises uncertainty and delays.

events.title: Reuters: drones also hit Kuwait power/water plant amid wider Gulf spillover
events.implication: Utilities disruption risk amplifies second-order impacts to industry and refining; adds to risk-off impulses.

events.title: UK hosts coalition talks with ~35 countries on reopening the Strait of Hormuz
events.implication: Signals widening diplomatic effort to restore navigation post-ceasefire; minesweeping and tanker protection planning suggests prolonged disruption risk.

events.title: Britain says ~40 countries discuss coalition options to reopen Strait of Hormuz after blockade
events.implication: Coalition planning (incl. mine-clearing/reassurance force) could reduce shipping risk but depends on end of hostilities.

events.title: Iranian fire hits U.S. A-10; aircraft crashes over Kuwait after engagement
events.implication: Highlights risk to coalition air operations and bases in Gulf states; may trigger additional strikes.

events.title: UK hosts virtual talks with ~35 countries on reopening the Strait of Hormuz
events.implication: Coalition planning suggests phased reopening (minesweeping then tanker protection), implying prolonged shipping constraints and elevated energy transport risk premia.

events.title: Britain hosts talks with ~40 countries on coalition to reopen Strait of Hormuz
events.implication: Coalition planning signals potential future escort/mine-clear operations; near-term risk remains high pending ceasefire.

events.title: Iran warns it will broaden attacks in response to U.S. threats, Reuters reports
events.implication: Escalatory messaging increases headline-driven volatility and raises probability of attacks on third-country assets.

events.title: Dozens of countries seek to restart Hormuz flows as Trump and Iran trade threats; Iran says it is drafting a permits/licensing protocol with Oman (Reuters)
events.implication: Reinforces risk that 'reopening' may involve new regulatory/fee regime; keeps disruption risk elevated.

events.title: Japanese-owned LNG tanker SOHAR LNG crosses the Strait of Hormuz; Japan says many Japan-linked ships remain stranded (Reuters)
events.implication: Selective crossings suggest negotiated/limited safe-passage is possible but overall disruption remains; supports volatile freight/insurance conditions.

events.title: Iran strikes power and water plant in Kuwait, Reuters reports
events.implication: Utilities/desalination vulnerability raises broader Gulf stability risk beyond energy assets.

events.title: UN Security Council to vote on Bahrain-drafted resolution on protecting shipping around the Strait of Hormuz
events.location_name: United Nations Headquarters, New York, United States
events.implication: Disagreement over authorizing force increases uncertainty about enforcement capacity, prolonging navigation risk and keeping an oil/shipping risk premium.

events.title: UK convenes ~35-country talks on coalition options to reopen the Strait of Hormuz (minesweeping, tanker protection)
events.implication: Signals coordinated planning to restore navigation; near-term supports risk of higher naval presence and shipping/insurance premia while plans are debated.

events.title: CMA CGM-owned container ship Kribi transits Strait of Hormuz; first French-owned vessel since war began
events.implication: A successful transit hints at potential de-escalation channels or negotiated exceptions; still high uncertainty.

events.title: UN Security Council to vote on Bahrain-drafted resolution to protect shipping in and around Hormuz
events.implication: Potential authorization for defensive measures affects legal basis for escorts; market sensitive to passage restoration prospects.

events.title: U.S. strike hits Tehran–Karaj B1 bridge; Iran state media reports casualties
events.implication: Attack on civilian infrastructure increases escalation risk and retaliation incentives.

events.title: Iran warns of 'broader and more destructive' attacks in response to U.S. threats
events.implication: Forward guidance of retaliation raises tail-risk for Gulf infrastructure and shipping.

events.title: French-owned CMA CGM container ship Kribi transits Strait of Hormuz after signaling 'Owner France'
events.implication: Demonstrates possible deconfliction/neutrality signaling; may encourage limited traffic but doesn’t normalize flows.

events.title: WTI prompt timespread hits record premium as market prices near-term shortage (Reuters)
events.implication: Curve backwardation signals immediate physical tightness expectations; can propagate into inflation pricing and equity sector rotation.

events.title: UK deploys Rapid Sentry air-defence system to Kuwait after overnight drone attack on a Kuwaiti oil facility (Reuters)
events.implication: Signals elevated expectation of follow-on drone threats; modestly supportive of risk premium by highlighting ongoing infrastructure exposure.

events.title: Philippines says Iran promised safe passage for Philippine ships and fuel flows via Hormuz (Reuters)
events.implication: Selective assurances imply a discretionary transit regime; uneven access can keep freight premia elevated.

events.title: Iranian fire hits two U.S. Blackhawks during pilot search; helicopters exit Iranian airspace
events.implication: Search-and-rescue operations under fire increase likelihood of broader engagement and miscalculation.

events.title: Iranian fire hits two U.S. Blackhawks during missing pilot search; helicopters exit Iran airspace
events.implication: Search-and-rescue operations face elevated attrition risk; may expand U.S. target set.

events.title: Israel strikes Beirut after warning residents to flee; U.S. warns Iran-aligned groups may target Lebanese universities
events.implication: Sustains multi-theatre conflict risk; increases probability of further regional spillover and risk-off moves.

events.title: Macron says opening Strait of Hormuz by force is unrealistic; reopening must be via diplomacy
events.implication: Signals limited appetite for military seizure; implies reopening likely slower and dependent on negotiation.

links.why: Coalition planning implies disruption is not imminently resolved; elevated uncertainty can drive episodic risk-off positioning.
